differential_diagnoses: Iron deficiency anemia (likely gastrointestinal blood loss)
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction
Chronotropic incompetence related to beta-blocker therapy
next_steps: Complete blood count with reticulocyte count
Iron studies (serum iron, total iron-binding capacity, ferritin)
Fecal occult blood test or diagnostic upper and lower endoscopy
Repeat transthoracic echocardiography to reassess aortic valve hemodynamics
B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) or NT-proBNP level
Cardiac stress testing or coronary angiography
Comprehensive metabolic panel and thyroid-stimulating hormone assessment

differential_diagnoses: Iron deficiency anemia secondary to chronic occult gastrointestinal blood loss
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction
Chronotropic incompetence due to beta-blocker therapy
next_steps: Complete blood count with differential and reticulocyte count
Repeat transthoracic echocardiography to assess valvular hemodynamic progression
Cardiac coronary CT angiography (avoiding stress testing due to severe stenosis)
Consultation for upper and lower endoscopy to evaluate for GI source of blood loss
